concept_query: Why do atoms form covalent bonds?

timeline.title: The Octet Rule Goal
timeline.voiceover_script: Atoms strive to reach stability. For most atoms, stability means having eight electrons in their outermost valence shell - just like noble gases.
visual_elements: Highlighting empty slots in outer electron shell
Noble gas neon blinking as stable standard

timeline.title: Sharing is Caring
timeline.voiceover_script: When non-metal atoms have similar electronegativities, neither wants to lose electrons. Instead, they share valence electrons, creating a mutual electrostatic attraction between their nuclei and the shared pair.
visual_elements: Two hydrogen atoms approaching each other
Overlapping electron shells forming a shared pair
Attractive force vectors between nuclei and electrons

timeline.title: Energy Stabilization
timeline.voiceover_script: By sharing electrons, atoms lower their potential energy to a minimum. The resulting dynamic overlap is what we call a stable covalent bond.
visual_elements: Energy graph dipping to minimum potential energy state
Completed stable H2 molecule icon with bond shown

meta_tags.note: Full LLM+TTS integration recommended for production. Use GPT-4 Vision for semantic validation and Runway/Sora for animated visuals.
